zh.lifeSummary: Jane Richard於1301年出生在英國米德萊可倫敦。她於1318年與Richard Empson在英國米德萊可倫敦結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世在英國英格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Jane Richard于1301年出生在英国米德萊可倫敦。她于1318年与Richard Empson在英国米德萊可倫敦结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世在英国英格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Emile, Jacques, Andre, Marcel, Pierre, Leonce, Normand, Camille, Lucien, Gilles, Henri.

English, French, West Indian (mainly Haiti), German, and Dutch: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements rīc ‘power(ful)’ + hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’. The Middle English forms were Richard and Rikard, respectively Parisian Old French and Old Picard pronunciations of the ancient Germanic name. Middle English Rikard gives rise to the pet form Rick , whilst Richard has given the pet forms Richie and Rich . Both produced rhyming pet forms, Dick and Hick for Rikard and Hitch for Richard.

History: The LA Richard families are mainly descended from Acadian refugees in the second half of the 18th century. — This surname is listed in the register of Huguenot ancestors recognized by the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Recker
meanings: North German, East Frisian, and Dutch: from a variant of the personal name   Richard  . This surname is very rare in East Frisia.
North German: topographic name from Middle Low German recke ‘stretch, route, hedge’, or a habitational name for someone from Recke, near Osnabrück.

variants.name: Ricard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Andre, Armand, Normand, Raoul, Adelard, Adrien, Amedee, Damien, Fernand, Francois, Germaine.
French: variant of   Richard  . Compare   Recor  ,   Record  ,   Recore  , and   Ricord  .
Channel Islands (Guernsey): variant of Rickard , the characteristic Norman pronunciation of Richard.

variants.name: Ricardo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Juan, Roberto, Carlos, Angel, Jorge, Ramon, Ediberto, Guillermo, Joaquin, Juana.
Potuguese and Spanish: from the personal name Ricardo, an equivalent of Richard .

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the name in 1 above at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Rick
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire and Lincolnshire): from the Middle English personal name Rick, a pet form of Richard . Compare Rich 2.
German: from a short form of any of the ancient Germanic personal names formed with rīc ‘power(ful)’.
North German: topographic name from Middle Low German ri(c)k ‘enclosed land, fence’, or from reke ‘row, strip of trees’.

variants.name: Riccardo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Salvatore, Angelo, Vito, Antonio, Carmine, Domenico, Francesca.
Italian: from the personal name Riccardo, an equivalent of Richard .

variants.name: Pritchard
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh ap Rhisiart ‘son of Richard’(see Richard ).

variants.name: Reick
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Helmuth.
German:
from a medieval personal name, a short form of   Richard  , or less commonly of some other compound name with this first element.

variants.name: Reichard
meanings: German: from an ancient Germanic personal name (see   Richard  ). This surname (and its variants) has also been adopted by Jews.

variants.name: Reisert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Mathias.
German: variant of Reissert, a variant of the ancient Germanic personal name Ricohard (see Richard ).

variants.name: Reitz
meanings: German: habitational name from a place near Stolpe in Pomerania called Reitz.
German: from a late medieval pet form of the personal name Richard or Heinrich (see Henry ).
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish female personal name Raytse, of ancient Germanic origin.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jaine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jane .

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Jane Richard was born in 1301, in London, Middlesex, England. She married Richard Empson in 1318, in London, Middlesex, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Jane Richard est née en 1301, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Richard Empson en 1318, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Jane Richard wurde 1301 in London, Middlesex, England geboren. Sie hat 1318 in London, Middlesex, England Richard Empson geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb in England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Jane Richard è nata il nel 1301 nel London, Middlesex, England. Lei sposato Richard Empson nel 1318, nel London, Middlesex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Richard(은)는 1301년 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1318년 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 Richard Empson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jane Richard nasceu em 1301, em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Richard Empson em 1318, em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Jane Richard родилась в 1301 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Empson в 1318 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла в местности Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Jane Richard nació en 1301, en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Se casó con Richard Empson en 1318, en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió en Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
